XRAY-962117 - CVE-2026-32280
- Go crypto/x509 Certificate.Verify slow chain building causing potential DoS in mTLS scenarios.
- Patched by upgrading Go to 1.26.2 in ark/Dockerfile, ark/Dockerfile.completions, images/ark-tools/Dockerfile and tools/fark/Dockerfile (1.25.9-alpine). Whitelist retained in case scanner caches previous scan images before rebuild.
XRAY-962422 - CVE-2026-39883
- OpenTelemetry-Go PATH hijacking via bare `kenv` command on BSD and Solaris platforms (fix in 1.43.0).
- Not exploitable in Ark: all Ark Go components run in Linux containers (alpine/bookworm). The `kenv` code path is only invoked on BSD/Solaris hosts. No BSD/Solaris runtime targets exist in Ark deployment.
XRAY-97724 - CVE-2018-20225
- An issue was discovered in pip (all versions) because it installs the version with the highest version number, even if the user had intended to obtain a private package from a private index. This only affects use of the --extra-index-url option, and exploitation requires that the package does not already exist in the public index (and thus the attacker can put the package there with an arbitrary version number). NOTE: it has been reported that this is intended functionality and the user is responsible for using --extra-index-url securely.
- In ARK pip is not used, but uv for fetching dependencies.
XRAY-913110 - CVE-2026-0621
- The npm package modelcontextprotocol/sdk has a vulnerability in versions 0 >= 1.25.1, however, 1.25.1 is the most up-to-date version. We will have to ignore this violation until a newer version is released.
- Used in ark-cli and filesystem-mcp
XRAY-934751 - CVE-2024-58340
- LangChain versions up to and including 0.3.1 contain a regular expression denial-of-service (ReDoS) vulnerability in the MRKLOutputParser.parse() method (libs/langchain/langchain/agents/mrkl/output_parser.py). The parser applies a backtracking-prone regular expression when extracting tool actions from model output. An attacker who can supply or influence the parsed text (for example via prompt injection in downstream applications that pass LLM output directly into MRKLOutputParser.parse()) can trigger excessive CPU consumption by providing a crafted payload, causing significant parsing delays and a denial-of-service condition.
- We appear to be using newer LangChain versions than the vulnerable oens and we aren't using the vulnerable method.
XRAY-935637 - CVE-2026-0994
- A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in google.protobuf (Python) versions up to 6.33.4 where json_format.ParseDict() allows max_recursion_depth bypass via nested google.protobuf.Any messages, causing Python recursion stack exhaustion. Severity: High (CVSS 8.2).
- No patched version is available yet. The fix is merged to protobuf master (PR #25239) but not released to PyPI. Latest available version is 6.33.4 (still vulnerable).
- Mitigations applied: (1) Pinned protobuf>=6.33.4 in ark-api and langchain-weather-agent, (2) Updated langchain-weather-agent from 6.33.0 to 6.33.4, (3) Documented vulnerability and upgrade path in CVE-2026-0994.md.
- Used in services/ark-api/ark-api and samples/a2a/langchain-weather-agent via a2a-sdk dependency.
- Will upgrade immediately when patched version is released to PyPI.
XRAY-937734 - CVE-2026-23864
- Resource exhaustion in React allows attackers to perform denial of service by sending crafted HTTP requests.
- Next.js is on the last version (16.1.6) and the fix is not yet available
- Used in docs only.
XRAY-589047 - CVE-2024-23342
- TODO: REMOVE THIS WHITELIST AFTER ARK-SDK VERSION IS BUMPED (currently 0.1.55)
- False positive: JFrog Xray matches ark-sdk:0.1.55 against PyPI registry and reports python-jose dependency from the published PyPI version, but our actual containers use a locally-built wheel with PyJWT instead of python-jose.
- The CVE affects python-ecdsa <= 0.18.0 via python-jose dependency. We migrated ark-sdk from python-jose to PyJWT (PR #1417) to fix this vulnerability.
- Evidence: (1) Local wheel metadata shows PyJWT[crypto]>=2.10.0, not python-jose. (2) Container build logs show pyjwt==2.12.1 being installed. (3) lib/ark-sdk/gen_sdk/overlay/python/pyproject.toml specifies PyJWT. (4) ark-sdk/auth/validator.py uses jwt.decode from PyJWT library.
- Scanner limitation: Xray matches package name+version to PyPI database instead of inspecting actual wheel contents. Since ark-sdk:0.1.55 exists on PyPI with python-jose, the scanner reports it despite our wheel having PyJWT.
- Mitigation: Next release will bump ark-sdk version, eliminating the PyPI version match and resolving the false positive.
- Affected images: ark-api, ark-evaluator, ark-mcp (all use locally-built ark-sdk wheel with PyJWT).
XRAY-944492 - CVE-2026-26996
- minimatch < 10.2.1 has ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) via glob patterns with many consecutive `*` wildcards followed by a non-matching literal, causing O(4^N) exponential backtracking. Severity: High (CVSS 8.7).
- Appears in ark-broker via swagger-jsdoc@6.2.8 -> glob@7.1.6 -> minimatch@3.1.3. The override in package.json pins minimatch to ^3.1.2 (latest 3.x), but 3.x is still in the affected range (< 10.2.1).
- Not exploitable in our context: swagger-jsdoc only uses glob at startup to scan for JSDoc annotations using hardcoded patterns (`./dist/**/*.js`, `./src/**/*.ts`). No user-controlled input reaches the minimatch pattern argument. The CVE requires attacker-controlled patterns with many consecutive `*` wildcards.
- Cannot upgrade: swagger-jsdoc@6.x depends on glob@7.x which requires minimatch@3.x API. minimatch@10.x is a complete rewrite with breaking API changes and cannot be used as a drop-in replacement. swagger-jsdoc has not released a version compatible with minimatch@10.x.
- Used in services/ark-broker/ark-broker only.
